title: gouldia osteocarpa
intro: Gouldia osteocarpa, also known as the mountain rose, is a small shrub native to New Caledonia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The extracts of Gouldia osteocarpa have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to promote immune function, reduce pain, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
